properties.name: Dismissible
properties.description: Determines wether the chip can be dismissed or not. The read-only variant can not be dismissible.

properties.name: Badge
properties.description: Can be used to attach a badge to the chip

properties.name: Value
properties.description: Controls what the value of the chips is

properties.name: Lead
properties.description: Controls what the lead of the chip is.

properties.name: Icon
properties.description: Controls the icon that is shown in the chip.

properties.name: Size
properties.description: Determines the vertical spacing of the chip. Dense can be used in situations where space is limited

properties.name: Color
properties.description: The color the chip should have. Be aware that some of the colors might be interpreted in a semantic way by users.

properties.name: State
properties.description: The state the button is currently in. The read-only variant does not have states.

properties.name: Variant
properties.description: Wether the chip is interactive or not. Read-only should be used for chips where no secondary action is needed in the lifecycle of the chip. For example to show the status of an entity.

table.name: Dismiss button
table.description: If present allows to dismiss the chip. For example if an applied filter needs to be removed.

table.name: Badge
table.description: The badge can be used to show numerical information. For example the amount of items which fall under a certain filter. See more in the badge documentation.

table.name: Value
table.description: The primary textual information that the chip represents.

table.name: Lead
table.description: If the value is of a certain category then the key can be used to show that category to provide additional context to the user.

table.name: Icon
table.description: The icon can be used to give more context to the chip. All icons from Vanilla are available. Note that semantic icons should only be used with the appropriate color (warning icon with yellow for example).
